snippets.title: Project layout (pytest auto-discovery)
snippets.code:
myproject/
├── src/
│   └── ragapp/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── chunker.py
│       └── retry.py
├── tests/
│   ├── test_chunker.py
│   └── test_retry.py
└── pyproject.toml
snippets.explanation: pytest discovers tests in files named `test_*.py` or `*_test.py`, in functions named `test_*`. Keep tests in `tests/` outside the src package.

snippets.title: A clean unit test (AAA pattern)
snippets.code:
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text, count_tokens


def test_chunk_text_respects_max_tokens():
    # Arrange
    text = "hello world " * 100
    max_tokens = 20

    # Act
    chunks = chunk_text(text, max_tokens=max_tokens)

    # Assert
    assert len(chunks) > 1
    assert all(count_tokens(c) <= max_tokens for c in chunks)
    assert "".join(chunks) == text  # lossless


def test_chunk_text_empty_input_returns_empty_list():
    assert chunk_text("", max_tokens=10) == []


def test_chunk_text_single_short_input_returns_one_chunk():
    assert chunk_text("hi", max_tokens=10) == ["hi"]
snippets.explanation: Short, named-by-behaviour, AAA-structured. A reviewer can scan all three in seconds.

snippets.title: Parametrized tests — many inputs, one body
snippets.code:
import pytest
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text_len, max_tokens, expected_chunks",
    [
        (0, 10, 0),
        (5, 10, 1),
        (10, 10, 1),  # exact-boundary case
        (11, 10, 2),  # just over
        (100, 10, 10),
    ],
)
def test_chunk_count_matches_expectation(text_len, max_tokens, expected_chunks):
    text = "a" * text_len
    chunks = chunk_text(text, max_tokens=max_tokens)
    assert len(chunks) == expected_chunks
snippets.explanation: Five tests for ~10 lines. Each row in the table is a separate test case — pytest reports them as `test_chunk_count_matches_expectation[0-10-0]`, etc.

snippets.title: Asserting exceptions and warnings
snippets.code:
import pytest
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text


def test_negative_max_tokens_raises():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="max_tokens must be positive"):
        chunk_text("hello", max_tokens=-1)


def test_zero_max_tokens_raises():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        chunk_text("hello", max_tokens=0)
snippets.explanation: `pytest.raises` catches the expected exception; `match=` checks the message with a regex.

snippets.title: Running the suite
snippets.code:
pytest                              # run everything
pytest tests/test_chunker.py        # one file
pytest -k chunk                     # tests whose name matches
pytest -x                           # stop on first failure
pytest -v                           # verbose: show each test name
pytest --lf                         # re-run last failed tests only
pytest -q --tb=short                # quiet output, short tracebacks (CI default)
snippets.explanation: `-x --lf` is the daily TDD loop: fix one test, see it pass, move on.
